grounding_note: Row 213 (disability overpayment) split raters among Consumer Law > Insurance, GL > Insurance (Health/Disability), and L&E Pensions/Benefits/ERISA because nothing said whether the policy was employer-sponsored, individually purchased, or ERISA-governed. Row 256 split Property Damage vs Insurance (Liability). The policy's institutional source and whose insurer denied what are facts the user can always disclose.

scenarios.opening_query: My long-term disability claim was cut off even though my doctors say nothing has changed.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The policy is part of my employer's group benefits plan — it came with my job and they deduct the premium from payroll.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's through my job — part of the group benefits plan, with the premium taken out of my paycheck.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the disability policy is employer-sponsored (ERISA) or individually purchased
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I bought the policy myself directly from the insurance company years ago; it has nothing to do with any job.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I bought it myself directly from the insurer — no employer involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 213's core ambiguity; ERISA preemption makes the plan source the single most consequential routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The insurance company denied my claim after our house fire and I don't know what to do next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the NEIGHBOR'S insurance that denied me — the fire started on his property, and I have uninsured losses I want him to cover.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was my neighbor's insurance that denied me — the fire started at his place and I want my uninsured losses covered.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the denial came from the user's own insurer or the responsible party's insurer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my own homeowner's insurer refusing to pay my valid claim under my own policy.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my own homeowner's company refusing to pay under my own policy.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 256 verbatim: 'sue neighbors insurance' actually means a tort claim against the neighbor; whose-policy is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Our health insurance won't cover the treatment my son's doctors say he needs. We're desperate.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: His coverage is through the state Medicaid plan, and the denial letter came from the state's managed care organization.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's on the state Medicaid plan — the denial came from their managed care organization.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the coverage is private insurance or a government health program
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's our private family plan through the marketplace — no government program involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's our private marketplace plan, not a government program.
scenarios.vetting_note: The taxonomy explicitly differentiates these by the government-entity fact; parents say 'our insurance' either way.

scenarios.opening_query: My insurance company is refusing to total my car correctly after the crash and lowballing me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Consumer Law > Insurance
scenarios.hidden_fact: It goes beyond my claim — I found out the agent sold me a policy tier that doesn't exist in their own filings, and the state has other complaints about the same scheme.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's worse than one claim — the agent sold me a coverage tier that doesn't even exist in their filings, and the state has other complaints about it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is one claim denial or a deceptive sales/business practice
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The policy itself is fine — they just refuse to pay what my car was worth on this claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The policy's fine — they just won't pay what the car was worth.
scenarios.vetting_note: Taxonomy separates bad-faith/deceptive practices (Consumer) from single claim denial (Litigation-Insurance); the pattern fact selects.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been on medical leave and the insurance handling my leave claim keeps stalling and asking for the same forms.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Labor & Employment > FMLA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: My employer is now saying my job isn't protected while I'm out — even though this was approved family medical leave for my surgery.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Now my employer claims my job isn't protected while I'm out — but this was approved family medical leave for surgery.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is with the insurer's payments or the employer's job protection under FMLA
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My job is safe — the fight is purely with the disability insurer, which stopped the wage-replacement checks.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My job's safe — it's the insurer that stopped the wage checks.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 335 verbatim posture: employer/insurer tangle; which entity is failing determines the exact route.

scenarios.opening_query: After the hailstorm our roof claim turned into a nightmare and we still have a tarp up there.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Consumer Law > Insurance
scenarios.hidden_fact: We learned the adjuster works on contingency to minimize payouts and told our contractor to falsify moisture readings — we recorded the call.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The adjuster gets paid to minimize payouts and told our contractor to falsify moisture readings — we have the recording.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether there is evidence of bad-faith claims-handling practices beyond a denial
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no funny business — they just dispute how much of the roof the hail damaged, and we disagree.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No funny business — we just disagree about how much of the roof was damaged.
scenarios.vetting_note: Documented bad-faith conduct vs valuation dispute; conduct evidence is the user-held fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The disability insurance people say we owe them $4,000 back in overpayments and they already sent it to collections.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee || General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The policy was bought individually through an insurance agent after my husband went self-employed — it was never connected to an employer.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He bought the policy himself through an agent when he went self-employed — it was never through any employer.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the disability policy is individual or employer-sponsored
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The policy came from his old company's group plan and the plan administrator is the one demanding repayment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's from his old company's group plan — the plan administrator is demanding the money.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 213's overpayment-to-collections scenario with the disambiguating source fact both directions.

scenarios.opening_query: My car was hit while parked and the other side's insurer says their driver isn't liable. The repair is $8,000.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || General Litigation > Insurance (Liability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I gave up on them and filed with my own insurer under my own collision coverage — and now MY company is refusing to pay too.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I filed under my own collision coverage instead — and now my own company won't pay either.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the current dispute is with the tortfeasor or the user's own insurer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I don't have collision coverage, so my only path is against the driver who hit me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I don't carry collision, so it's the other driver or nothing.
scenarios.vetting_note: First-party vs third-party posture changes the label; users move between them without noticing.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's life insurance claim was denied after he passed and I can't get anyone on the phone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the group life policy from his employer's benefits package — the employer's plan administrator handled the paperwork.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the group life policy from his job's benefits package — their plan administrator handled everything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the life policy is an employer group benefit governed by ERISA
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private term life policy he bought on his own twenty years ago.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the private term policy he bought himself twenty years ago.
scenarios.vetting_note: Widow intake; ERISA vs state-law bad faith rides entirely on the plan-source fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Our insurance rates tripled and then they dropped us entirely right after we filed our first claim ever.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Insurance || General Litigation > Insurance (Liability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bigger problem is the claim itself — the water damage from the burst pipe was never paid, $32,000, and the denial reasons keep changing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The real fight is the claim — $32,000 in water damage they never paid, and their denial reasons keep changing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the core dispute is the unpaid claim or the rate/cancellation practices
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They actually paid the claim — my issue is the retaliatory cancellation and what I've learned they do to everyone who files.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They paid the claim — my problem is the retaliatory cancellation they apparently do to everyone who files.
scenarios.vetting_note: Coverage dispute vs unfair-practices dispute distinguishes the two insurance labels the taxonomy separates.
